documentation.value: Retrieves the string value stored at the specified key.
The key name to retrieve.
Sets a key to hold a string value, with optional expiration and conditional flags.
The key name.
The string value to store.
Optional. Set key expiration in seconds.
NX: set only if key doesn't exist. XX: set only if key exists.
Deletes one or more keys. Returns the number of keys removed.
One or more key names to delete.
Returns the value of a field in a hash.
The hash key.
The field within the hash.
Sets one or more field-value pairs in a hash.
The hash key.
The field name.
The value for the field.
Prepends one or more elements to the head of a list.
The list key.
One or more elements to push to the left.
Appends one or more elements to the tail of a list.
The list key.
One or more elements to push to the right.
Returns a range of elements from a list. Use 0 -1 for full list.
The list key.
The start index (0-based).
The stop index (inclusive, -1 for last).
Returns all keys matching the glob-style pattern. Avoid in production; use SCAN instead.
The pattern to match (e.g., *, user:*, session:????).
Streams back every command processed by the Redis server in real time. Useful for debugging. Press Ctrl+C to stop.
Subscribes the client to one or more channels for Pub/Sub messaging.
One or more channel names to subscribe to.
Posts a message to a Pub/Sub channel. Returns the number of clients that received the message.
The channel to publish to.
The message to send.
